[![Review Change Stack](https://storage.googleapis.com/coderabbit_public_assets/review-stack-in-coderabbit-ui.svg)](https://app.coderabbit.ai/change-stack/cesarnml/son-of-anton/pull/24)
<!-- walkthrough_start -->

## Walkthrough

This PR extends the delivery orchestrator CLI to support runtime policy overrides for review behavior. New CLI flags (`--subagent-review-policy`, `--pr-review-policy`, `--review-subagent`, `--same-review-subagent`) are added to `parseCliArgs()` with validation against policy stage values and mutual exclusivity checks. A new exported function `resolveRuntimePolicyOverrides()` centralizes how these CLI flags patch the runtime config, merging review policies and computing subagent overrides while enforcing prerequisites like `prReviewAgents` presence. The orchestrator's `start` command conditionally re-stamps persisted `runPolicy` from the resolved config only when explicit policy flags are supplied, preserving carry-forward behavior otherwise. State normalization is refined to distinguish null from undefined run policy values.

<!-- walkthrough_end -->

> - Investigate incidents and troubleshoot customer tickets together
> - Automate recurring tasks and respond to alerts with triggers

> 
> Built for teams:
> 
> - **Shared memory** across your entire org—no repeating context
> - **Per-thread sandboxes** to safely plan and execute work
> - **Governance built-in**—scoped access, auditability, and budget controls
> 
> One agent for your entire SDLC. Right inside Slack.

> </details>

ⓘ You've reached your Qodo monthly free-tier limit. Reviews pause until next month — upgrade your plan to continue now, or link your paid account if you already have one.

**Actionable comments posted: 1**

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for all review comments with AI agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

Inline comments:
In `@tools/delivery/cli.ts`:
- Around line 203-213: The current argv parsing for the --review-subagent flag
accepts whitespace-only values (raw) which violates the "non-blank agent
identifier" contract; update the check in the CLI parsing logic so after reading
raw (the next argv element) you validate using raw.trim() and throw the same
error if raw is missing or raw.trim() === '' ; then assign reviewSubagent =
raw.trim() (instead of raw) and increment index as before. Ensure you reference
the variables argv, raw, reviewSubagent and index in the changed block.
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🪄 Autofix (Beta)</summary>

Fix all unresolved CodeRabbit comments on this PR:

- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "4b0d0e0a-96d7-4f10-b296-3a18ea78f0b9"} --> Push a commit to this branch (recommended)
- [ ] <!-- {"checkboxId": "ff5b1114-7d8c-49e6-8ac1-43f82af23a33"} --> Create a new PR with the fixes

</details>
